title: lint: reenable staticcheck
body:
It was disabled in #2625 ("Use same linters as podman"), but
unfortunately with no explanation.

That PR removed a lot more linters. Someone should probably
go through, reevaluate & reenable.

[ Reference: #4880 ]

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Tip (if you push again): Probably no need to run the full CI suite for this.  I think you can `[CI:DOCS]` this since golangci-lint always runs.

> unfortunately with no explanation.

Clearly that was my fault.  I don't remember for sure, but most likely I simply copied the config. from podman w/o looking twice.  

In case it helps, my best guess is:  Frequently, golangci-lint linters are a major PITA to debug when they don't work.  They tend to fail in either a panic, hang or a non-zero exit w/o any message or explanation.  It's quite frustrating :angry:

It's not a fault thing, have you looked at the histories of `.golang-ci.yml` in podman and buildah? (Suggestion: don't). Combine new VMs, plus new Go versions, plus time pressures, plus best intentions, and important things get dropped by the wayside. 

Thanks for the CI-DOC hint, but I'm still wondering why CI is passing (so far) on this. I was expecting a lint failure.

> It's not a fault thing,

No worries, yep it's a complete mess.  Just trying to add some context in case it helps.

> I was expecting a lint failure.

Maybe this is related to the "Not the same thing as the staticcheck binary" note?

Oof, found another [confusing link to staticcheck.io](https://golangci-lint.run/usage/linters/#staticcheck) as well.  Maybe it is the same thing and the golangci-lint readme comment is wrong? :man_shrugging: 

> I was expecting a lint failure.

Oh, maybe @testwill used a different/newer version than what comes in with golangci-lint (assuming it's even the same tool)?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Do not merge. This should not have passed. I need to understand why it did.

TL;DR `golangci-lint staticcheck` != `staticcheck`; having it enabled would not have caught the bug fixed in #4880.

----------
Full details: as discovered by @cevich, [golangci-lint staticcheck](https://golangci-lint.run/usage/linters/) is something completely different from the [staticcheck that caught the dup import](https://staticcheck.dev/docs/getting-started/). The latter is a standalone tool. It is what caught the dup-import bug:
```
run.go:12:2: package "github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/specs-go" is being imported more than once (ST1019)
run_common.go:50:2: [ibid]
run_linux.go:42:2: [ibid]
```
Perhaps the standalone `staticcheck` tool would be a good addition to our CI, but that is beyond the scope of what I had set out to do. Should anyone be interested in taking it on, please note that the following other issues will need to be fixed or waived:
```
add.go:468:22: pm.Matches is deprecated: Please use the `MatchesResult` method instead. Matches matches path against all the patterns. Matches is not safe to be called concurrently  (SA1019)
import.go:22:15: error strings should not be capitalized (ST1005)
run_common.go:1626:19: error strings should not be capitalized (ST1005)
run_linux.go:419:18: hooksExec.RuntimeConfigFilter is deprecated: Too many arguments, has been refactored and replaced by RuntimeConfigFilterWithOptions instead  (SA1019)
```

> that is beyond the scope of what I had set out to do

I can look into this.  I've got a PR already open to build new images, should be easy to drop in a tool like this.  Alternatively, this took strikes me as something somebody has probably already written a github-action for.  Should be easy to just drop that in.
Ahh, yep, [there's an offical GHA for it](https://staticcheck.dev/docs/running-staticcheck/ci/github-actions/).
